```xml
	<flow name="Get_Profile_Metadata" >
		<http:listener doc:name="GET /profile/metadata/{dataModelName}" allowedMethods="GET" path="/profile/metadata/{dataModelName}" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"/>
		<sdc:get-meta doc:name="Profile - Get Metadata" config-ref="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" dataModelNameUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.dataModelName]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Test the flow by sending a `GET` to `localhost:8081/profile/metadata/{DATA_MODEL_NAME}`.

## Profile - Search Records API

This Mule flow shows how to retrieve data model object records based on search filters. Refer to the
[Salesforce Data Cloud API Developer Guide](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/data/data-cloud-query-guide/references/data-cloud-query-api-reference/c360a-api-profile-dmname.html)
for usage and further details.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts HTTP GET requests with the data model name as the required URI parameter, fields as the
  required query parameter, and a combination of any other query parameters.
- **Profile - Search Records**
  - Authenticates based on your choice of OAuth Client Credentials, OAuth JWT, or OAuth Username and
    Password.
  - Receives the data model name, fields and any optional query parameters.
  - Calls the Salesforce Data Cloud API to return the data model object records based on search
    filters.

![Salesforce Data Cloud Profile Search Records flow with Listener and Profile Search Records](_images/profile-search-records.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:sdc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc/current/mule-sdc.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce/current/mule-salesforce.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-jwt-connection consumerKey="${server.consumerKey}" keyStorePath="${server.keyStorePath}" storePassword="${server.keyStorePassword}" subject="${server.userName}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" keyAlias="${server.certificateAlias}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<configuration-properties doc:name="Configuration properties" file="mule-app.properties" />
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_UsernamePassword_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-user-pass-connection clientId="${server.consumerKey}" clientSecret="${server.consumerSecret}" username="${server.userName}" password="${server.password}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<flow name="Search_Profile_Records" >
		<http:listener doc:name="GET /profile/{dataModelName}" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/profile/{dataModelName}"/>
		<sdc:get-parent-with-filters doc:name="Profile - Search Records"  config-ref="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" dataModelNameUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.dataModelName]" orderbyQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.orderby]" filtersQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.filters]" fieldsQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.fields]" limitQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.limit]" offsetQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.offset]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Test the flow by sending a `GET` to
    `localhost:8081/profile/{DATA_MODEL_NAME}?filters={FILTERS}`.

## Profile - Search Records By ID API

This Mule flow shows how to retrieve data model object records based on search indexes and filters.
Refer to the
[Salesforce Data Cloud API Developer Guide](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/data/data-cloud-query-guide/references/data-cloud-query-api-reference/c360a-api-profile-dmname-id.html)
for usage and further details.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts HTTP GET requests with the data model name and ID as the required URI parameters, and a
  combination of any optional query parameters.
- **Profile - Search Records By Id**
  - Authenticates based on your choice of OAuth Client Credentials, OAuth JWT, or OAuth Username and
    Password.
  - Receives the data model name, ID, and any optional query parameters.
  - Calls the Salesforce Data Cloud API to return the data model object records based on search
    indexes and filters.

![Salesforce Data Cloud Profile Search Records By Id flow with Listener and Profile Search Records By Id](_images/profile-search-records-by-id.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:sdc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc/current/mule-sdc.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce/current/mule-salesforce.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-jwt-connection consumerKey="${server.consumerKey}" keyStorePath="${server.keyStorePath}" storePassword="${server.keyStorePassword}" subject="${server.userName}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" keyAlias="${server.certificateAlias}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<configuration-properties doc:name="Configuration properties" file="mule-app.properties" />
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_UsernamePassword_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-user-pass-connection clientId="${server.consumerKey}" clientSecret="${server.consumerSecret}" username="${server.userName}" password="${server.password}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<flow name="Search_Profile_Records_By_Id" >
		<http:listener doc:name="GET /profile/{dataModelName}/{Id}" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/profile/{dataModelName}/{Id}"/>
		<sdc:get-parent doc:name="Profile - Search Records By Id" config-ref="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" dataModelNameUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.dataModelName]" idUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.Id]" searchKeyQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.searchKey]" fieldsQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.fields]" filtersQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.filters]" limitQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.limit]" orderbyQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.orderby]" offsetQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.offset]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Test the flow by sending a `GET` to `localhost:8081/profile/{DATA_MODEL_NAME}/{Id}`.

## Profile - Search Records With Child Records API

This Mule flow shows how to retrieve data model object records along with child data model object
records based on indexes and search filters. Refer to the
[Salesforce Data Cloud API Developer Guide](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/data/data-cloud-query-guide/references/data-cloud-query-api-reference/c360a-api-profile-dmname-id-child-dmname.html)
for usage and further details.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts HTTP GET requests with the data model name, child data model name, and ID as the required
  URI parameters, and a combination of any optional query parameters.
- **Profile - Search Records With Child Records**
  - Authenticates based on your choice of OAuth Client Credentials, OAuth JWT, or OAuth Username and
    Password.
  - Receives the data model name, child data model name, ID, and any optional query parameters.
  - Calls the Salesforce Data Cloud API to return the data model object records along with child
    data model object records based on indexes and search filters.

![Salesforce Data Cloud Profile Search Records With Child Records flow with Listener and Profile Search Records With Child Records](_images/profile-search-records-with-child-records.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:sdc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc/current/mule-sdc.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce/current/mule-salesforce.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-jwt-connection consumerKey="${server.consumerKey}" keyStorePath="${server.keyStorePath}" storePassword="${server.keyStorePassword}" subject="${server.userName}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" keyAlias="${server.certificateAlias}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<configuration-properties doc:name="Configuration properties" file="mule-app.properties" />
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_UsernamePassword_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-user-pass-connection clientId="${server.consumerKey}" clientSecret="${server.consumerSecret}" username="${server.userName}" password="${server.password}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<flow name="Search_Profile_Records_With_Child_Records" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Get /profile/{dataModelName}/{id}/{childDataModelName}" allowedMethods="GET" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/profile/{dataModelName}/{id}/{childDataModelName}"/>
		<sdc:get-parent-and-child doc:name="Profile - Search Records With Child Records" searchKeyQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.searchKey]" fieldsQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.fields]" limitQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.limit]" filtersQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.filters]" offsetQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.offset]" orderbyQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.orderby]" dataModelNameUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.dataModelName]" idUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.id]" childDataModelNameUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.childDataModelName]" config-ref="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Test the flow by sending a `GET` to
    `localhost:8081/profile/{DATA_MODEL_NAME}/{Id}/{CHILD_DATA_MODEL_NAME}`.

## Profile - Search Records With Insight API

This Mule flow shows how to retrieve data model object records and a computed view based on indexes
and search filters. Refer to the
[Salesforce Data Cloud API Developer Guide](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/data/data-cloud-query-guide/references/data-cloud-query-api-reference/c360a-api-profile-dmname-id-ci-ci-name.html)
for usage and further details.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts HTTP GET requests with the data model name, ID, and calculated insight name as the
  required URI parameters, and a combination of any optional query parameters.
- **Profile - Search Records With Insight**
  - Authenticates based on your choice of OAuth Client Credentials, OAuth JWT, or OAuth Username and
    Password.
  - Receives the data model name, ID, calculated insight name, and any optional query parameters.
  - Calls the Salesforce Data Cloud API to return the data model object records and a computed view
    based on indexes and search filters.

![Salesforce Data Cloud Profile Search Records With Insight flow with Listener and Profile Search Records With Insight](_images/profile-search-records-with-insight.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:sdc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc/current/mule-sdc.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce/current/mule-salesforce.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-jwt-connection consumerKey="${server.consumerKey}" keyStorePath="${server.keyStorePath}" storePassword="${server.keyStorePassword}" subject="${server.userName}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" keyAlias="${server.certificateAlias}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<configuration-properties doc:name="Configuration properties" file="mule-app.properties" />
	<sdc:sdc-config name="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_UsernamePassword_config" doc:name="Salesforce Data Cloud config" >
		<sdc:oauth-user-pass-connection clientId="${server.consumerKey}" clientSecret="${server.consumerSecret}" username="${server.userName}" password="${server.password}" audienceUrl="${server.audienceUrl}" />
	</sdc:sdc-config>
	<flow name="Search_Profile_Records_With_Insight" >
		<http:listener doc:name="GET /profile/{dataModelName}/{id}/calculated-insights/{ci_name}" allowedMethods="GET" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/profile/{dataModelName}/{id}/calculated-insights/{ci_name}"/>
		<sdc:get-computed-view-for-profile doc:name="Profile - Search Records With Insight" config-ref="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_OAuth_JWT_config" dataModelNameUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.dataModelName]" idUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.id]" ciNameUriParam="#[attributes.uriParams.ci_name]" searchKeyQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.searchKey]" dimensionsQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.dimensions]" measuresQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.measures]" limitQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.limit]" filtersQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.filters]" offsetQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.offset]" orderbyQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.orderby]" timeGranularityQueryParam="#[attributes.queryParams.timeGranularity]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  Test the flow by sending a `GET` to
    `localhost:8081/profile/{DATA_MODEL_NAME}/{Id}/calculated-insights/{CALCULATED_INSIGHT_NAME}`.

## Data Action Webhook

This Mule flow shows how to create a webhook that logs its payload when triggered by a Data Action.
Refer to the
[Salesforce Data Action docs](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=data.c360_a_data_actions.htm&type=5).

This example uses the following operations:

- **Data Action Webhook**  
  Creates a webhook as the target of a Salesforce Data Cloud Data Action.
- **Logger**  
  Shows the payload sent to the Data Action Webhook.

![Salesforce Data Cloud Data Action Webhook flow with Data Cloud Data Action Webhook and Logger Log Payload](_images/data-action-webhook.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:sdc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
    xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
    xsi:schemaLocation="
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/sdc/current/mule-sdc.xsd">
	<flow name="Data_Action_Webhook" doc:id="3072197f-ee1f-4ed5-99d2-a9fa62230dbf" >
		<sdc:webhook-listener doc:name="Data Action Webhook" doc:id="fe3a924a-ba94-447d-9d3c-df079fc01de2" config-ref="Salesforce_Data_Cloud_Sdc_webhook_config" path="${webhook.path}" signingKey="${webhook.signingKey}" signingAlgorithm="HmacSHA256"/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Payload" doc:id="716538d3-7d06-4e9c-a4f7-b0d350b4dafe" message="#[output application/json --- payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  [Create a Streaming Insight](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=data.c360_a_create_streaming_insight.htm&type=5).
2.  [Create a Data Action Target](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=data.c360_a_data_actions.htm&type=5)
    with an Action Target Type of `Webhook`.
3.  [Create a Data Action](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=data.c360_a_data_actions.htm&type=5)
    that uses the Data Action Target and runs on a condition against your streaming Calculated
    Insight.
4.  Save the Mule project.
5.  [Deploy your app to CloudHub.](../../cloudhub/deploying-to-cloudhub)
6.  Point the data action target to your applicationâs URL.

    Your application URL looks like this:
    `[https://{{app-name}}.{{region}}.cloudhub.io/webhook](https://{{app-name}}.{{region}}.cloudhub.io/webhook)`.

7.  Trigger the data actionâs condition to test the flow, and observe the logged payload.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Salesforce Data Cloud Connector 1.4 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Data Cloud (Salesforce Data Cloud Connector) provides Mule users
with a quick and easy way to integrate with Salesforce Data Cloud API endpoints. Users can automate
data ingestion from third parties, query the Data Cloud Data Lake, search and query customer profile
information and calculated insights, and consume data action events to enrich the 360-degree view of
their customers in their Data Cloud instance.

## Configurations

---

### SDC Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#SdcConfig_OauthClientCredentials">OAuth Client Credentials</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#SdcConfig_OauthJwt">OAuth JWT</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#SdcConfig_OauthUserPass">OAuth Username Password</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures an expiration policy for the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### OAuth Client Credentials

Custom connection provider for the OAuth Client Credentials flow.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Headers automatically added to every outbound request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Query parameters automatically added to every outbound request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when initially establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Your applicationâs client secret (consumer secret in Remote Access Detail).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL pointing to the server responsible for providing the authentication token.</p></td><td><p><code>https://domain.my.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data Space</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results from this data space only.</p></td><td><p>default</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are <code>HTTP</code> and <code>HTTPS</code>.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OAuth JWT

Custom connection provider for the OAuth JWT flow.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Headers automatically added to every outbound request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Query parameters automatically added to every outbound request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when initially establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the keystore used to sign data during authentication.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Store password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password of the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Certificate alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the certificate.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Subject</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Audience url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifies the authorization server as an intended audience.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data Space</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results from this data space only.</p></td><td><p>default</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are <code>HTTP</code> and <code>HTTPS</code>.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OAuth Username Password

Custom connection provider for the username and password flow.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Headers automatically added to every outbound request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><p>Query parameters automatically added to every outbound request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when initially establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether to use persistent connections:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Mule uses persistent connections.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Mule closes the connection after the first request completes.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections to open to the backend. HTTP requests are sent in parallel over multiple connections. Setting this value too high can impact latency and consume additional resources without increasing throughput.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>When persistent connections are enabled, how long a connection can remain idle before Mule closes it.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If this value is <code>true</code>, Mule streams received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Size of the buffer that stores the HTTP response, in bytes.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce-connected app.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Your applicationâs client secret (consumer secret in Remote Access Detail).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username used to initialize the session.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to authenticate the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Audience url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Identifies the authorization server as an intended audience.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data Space</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Filters results from this data space only.</p></td><td><p>default</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are <code>HTTP</code> and <code>HTTPS</code>.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Data Action Webhook Config

Configuration element for a Webhook Source.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SdcWebhookConfig_Listener">Listener</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base path to use for all requests that reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of this element used for reference in other components</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Listener

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>TLS_Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Reference to a TLS configuration element. Enables HTTPS for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. When using HTTPS, the HTTP communication is secured using TLS or SSL. If HTTPS is configured as the protocol, the user must configure the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of this listener configuration.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host in which the requests are sent.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port in which the requests are received.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>false</code>, each connection is closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of milliseconds that a connection can remain idle before it is closed. The value of this attribute is used only when persistent connections are enabled. The listener default timeout is bigger than our requester default timeout to avoid the <code>Remotely closed</code> exception when sending a request to an existing connection before the timeout occurs.</p></td><td><p>40000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Bulk - Upload Job Data](#UploadDataBulkJob)

## Associated Sources

- [Data Action Webhook](#WebhookSource)

## Operations

## Bulk - Create Job

`<sdc:create-bulk-job>`

Creates a job that represents the bulk operation for sending data to Salesforce Data Cloud for
asynchronous processing. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /ingest/jobs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source API name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the ingestion source API to work with.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of object to work with.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Operation</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>delete</p></li><li><p>upsert</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Operation to execute.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SDC Config](#SdcConfig)

### Throws

- SDC:BAD_REQUEST
- SDC:CLIENT_ERROR
- SDC:CONNECTIVITY
- SDC:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SDC:NOT_FOUND
- SDC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SDC:SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SDC:TIMEOUT
- SDC:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SDC:UNAUTHORIZED
- SDC:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Bulk - Delete Job

`<sdc:delete-bulk-job>`

Deletes the job for the specified job ID. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/ingest/jobs/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Job ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Job ID returned in the response body from the <strong>Create Job</strong> request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SDC Config](#SdcConfig)

### Throws

- SDC:BAD_REQUEST
- SDC:CLIENT_ERROR
- SDC:CONNECTIVITY
- SDC:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SDC:NOT_FOUND
- SDC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SDC:SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SDC:TIMEOUT
- SDC:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SDC:UNAUTHORIZED
- SDC:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Streaming - Delete Objects

`<sdc:delete-objects>`

Deletes one or more records from Salesforce Data Cloud. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request
to the /ingest/sources/{source-name}/{object-name} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source API name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Ingest source API name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the collection to delete data from.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>IDs</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of IDs to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SDC Config](#SdcConfig)

### Throws

- SDC:BAD_REQUEST
- SDC:CLIENT_ERROR
- SDC:CONNECTIVITY
- SDC:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SDC:NOT_FOUND
- SDC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SDC:SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SDC:TIMEOUT
- SDC:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SDC:UNAUTHORIZED
- SDC:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Insights - List Metadata

`<sdc:get-all-calculated-insight-metadata>`

The metadata includes the dimension and measure that are part of the calculated insight. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /insight/metadata endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SDC Config](#SdcConfig)

### Throws

- SDC:BAD_REQUEST
- SDC:CLIENT_ERROR
- SDC:CONNECTIVITY
- SDC:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SDC:NOT_FOUND
- SDC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SDC:SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SDC:TIMEOUT
- SDC:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SDC:UNAUTHORIZED
- SDC:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Bulk - Get Job

`<sdc:get-bulk-job>`

Retrieves the job details for the specified job ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
ingest/jobs/{id} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Job ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Job ID returned in the response body from the <strong>Create Job</strong> request.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SDC Config](#SdcConfig)

### Throws

- SDC:BAD_REQUEST
- SDC:CLIENT_ERROR
- SDC:CONNECTIVITY
- SDC:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SDC:NOT_FOUND
- SDC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SDC:SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SDC:TIMEOUT
- SDC:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SDC:UNAUTHORIZED
- SDC:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Bulk - List Jobs

`<sdc:get-bulk-jobs>`

Retrieves all bulk jobs. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /ingest/jobs endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SDC Config](#SdcConfig)

### Throws

- SDC:BAD_REQUEST
- SDC:CLIENT_ERROR
- SDC:CONNECTIVITY
- SDC:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SDC:NOT_FOUND
- SDC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SDC:SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SDC:TIMEOUT
- SDC:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SDC:UNAUTHORIZED
- SDC:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Insights - Get Metadata

`<sdc:get-calculated-insight-metadata>`

The metadata includes the dimension and measure that are part of the calculated insight. This
operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /insight/metadata/{ci-name} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ci_name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calculated insight name, for example, <code>IndividualChildrenCount__cio</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long Mule waits for a response to complete before the response times out.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SDC Config](#SdcConfig)

### Throws

- SDC:BAD_REQUEST
- SDC:CLIENT_ERROR
- SDC:CONNECTIVITY
- SDC:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SDC:NOT_FOUND
- SDC:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SDC:SERVER_ERROR
- SDC:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SDC:TIMEOUT
- SDC:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SDC:UNAUTHORIZED
- SDC:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Insights - Get Insights

`<sdc:get-calculated-insight-with-filters-fields-and-limit>`

Use this API to query the calculated insights. Users can slice, dice, and filter by selecting
different dimensions, measures, and filters. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/insight/calculated-insights/{ci-name} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>ci_name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Calculated insight name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>dimensions</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of dimensions to project. By default, this parameter includes all of the available dimensions.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>measures</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of measures to project. By default, this parameter includes all of the available measures.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of items to return. The maximum number of items to return is 4999.</p></td><td><p>4999</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of rows to skip before starting to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>filters</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter is added to a select query to select only results of a specific type.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>orderby</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated values to sort the result set in ascending or descending order.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>timeGranularity</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HOUR</p></li><li><p>DAY</p></li><li><p>MONTH</p></li><li><p>QUARTER</p></li><li><p>YEAR</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>User-specified granularity if <strong>ci</strong> has one or more time dimensions available.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request.
